name: Jeff "Tain" Watts
abstract: Jeff "Tain" Watts (born January 20, 1960) is an American jazz drummer and composer who has worked across genres including Grammy wins with The Metropolitan Opera,  Wynton Marsalis, Branford Marsalis Quartet and Mingus Big Band. Performing with Michael Brecker, Alice Coltrane, Kenny Garrett, McCoy Tyner, Ravi Coltrane, and others.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Young New Orleans
JVC Jazz Festival: Wynton Marsalis Salutes Dizzy Gillespie on His Seventieth Birthday

works.t: Champion: What makes a man a man?
Champion: Champagne dreams and lime juice money

about_quotes.speaker: Delfeayo Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: he said, 'I’m going to do a record with Milt Hinton and Tain' [Jeff “Tain” Watts]. That was the record that changed everything
about_quotes.doc_title: Delfeayo Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Tain and I have a thirty-year personal and working relationship that’s very amazing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I actually did have one drum lesson with Jeff “Tain” Watts. We hung out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing at a jazz festival in Boise, Idaho. Michael Brecker was the headliner. Michael was brilliant. He had Joey Calderazzo on piano, Reginald Veal on bass, and Jeff Watts on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Delf’s like, “Yeah man. Got this tape of a gig that Branford did with his new band with Kenny Kirkland and Jeff Watts and Bob Hurst.” I was like, okay.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: people like Wynton Marsalis and Jeff Watts, who when they came on the scene... they used that rhythm a lot
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002
